column1: # eq_hist4_war_on_drugs_capacity.csv — Incarceration and enforcement disparities
# Sources: Bureau of Justice Statistics (BJS) — National Prisoner Statistics Program
#          https://bjs.ojp.gov/data-collection/national-prisoner-statistics-nps-program
#          ACLU (2020) "A Tale of Two Countries" — cannabis arrest racial disparities
#          https://www.aclu.org/report/tale-two-countries-racially-targeted-arrests-era-marijuana-reform
#          The Sentencing Project — Trends in U.S. Corrections: State-by-State Incarceration Rates by Race/Ethnicity
#          https://www.sentencingproject.org/reports/
#          Lead data: Nevin (2007) "Understanding international crime trends: The legacy of preschool lead exposure"
#          Environmental Research 104(3):315-336. doi:10.1016/j.envres.2007.02.008
#          Reyes (2007) "Environmental Policy as Social Policy? The Impact of Childhood Lead Exposure on Crime"
#          B.E. Journal of Economic Analysis & Policy 7(1). doi:10.2202/1935-1682.1796
#          CDC NHANES I (1976-80)
#          CDC MMWR 2013 "Blood lead levels in children aged 1-5 years — United States
# Note: incarceration rates are per 100
#       drug_offense_share = proportion of sentenced prisoners serving time for drug offenses
#       capacity_factor = (1 - ratio-normalized enforcement burden) — net capacity retained by O_racialized
#       black_lead_lag20_ugdl / white_lead_lag20_ugdl = geometric mean blood lead level (µg/dL) of
#         children aged 1-5 measured at (year - 20)
column3: the cohort reaching ~age 20 in the data year.
